docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2982:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2983:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:64:`_lockWheatPlotsForWinter` *unconditionally* sets every plot's `state = WinterLocked` regardless of pre-existing state. If a plot was `Regrowing` with `regrowUntilTick <= 109` (pre-winter), and the clan didn't trigger `_settleClan` in the [regrow-end, winter-start] window, the lazy regrow check (`if (plot.state == WheatPlotState.Regrowing && tick >= plot.regrowUntilTick)`) at line 379 will never see `Regrowing` — it's already been overwritten to `WinterLocked` by the heartbeat. The "should-have-been-Harvestable" tick is silently dropped.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2984:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:66:Practical impact is bounded: post-winter `_restartWheatPlotsAfterWinter` resets all plots to a fresh `Regrowing(winterEnd + 4)`, so the only damage is lost harvest *opportunity* in the pre-winter regrow window for clans that didn't settle. With orchestrator polling every tick the window is small. Still, fairness asymmetry between settled/unsettled clans is real.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2986:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:143:`ClanWorld.sol:1161-1165`. Iterates all clans, including DEAD ones. Resetting `coldDamage = 0` on a dead clan is harmless (the field is unread for dead clans). Wasted gas at winter-end heartbeat. Add `if (clan.clanState == ClanState.DEAD) continue;`. Same for `_lockWheatPlotsForWinter` and `_restartWheatPlotsAfterWinter` (lines 1132-1158) — locking/unlocking a dead clan's plots writes to slots that will never be read again. Trivial gas cleanup.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2987:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3041:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3293:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10070:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1132-1140` locks wheat plots for winter without clearing `remainingWheat` or resetting regrow state, and the tests at `packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2069-2074` and `:2125-2128` explicitly assert that preserved-state behavior. That disagrees with `docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:843-846`, which says winter start clears all plots. Harvest is blocked, so this is mostly a state/spec mismatch today, but getters will expose impossible in-winter crop state and future logic can accidentally depend on it.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3294:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10093:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1132-1140` locks wheat plots for winter without clearing `remainingWheat` or resetting regrow state, and the tests at `packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2069-2074` and `:2125-2128` explicitly assert that preserved-state behavior. That disagrees with `docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:843-846`, which says winter start clears all plots. Harvest is blocked, so this is mostly a state/spec mismatch today, but getters will expose impossible in-winter crop state and future logic can accidentally depend on it.

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3636:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:418:- at winter end: `WinterLocked -> Regrowing`
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3946:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9028:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3947:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9032:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4140:        clan.starvationStartsAtTick = 0;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4229:            // reset winter timers for new season
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4230:            _world.winterActive = false;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4231:            _world.winterStartsAtTick = _world.seasonStartTick + ClanWorldConstants.TICKS_PER_WINTER_CYCLE
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4233:            _world.winterEndsAtTick = _world.seasonStartTick + ClanWorldConstants.TICKS_PER_WINTER_CYCLE;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4236:        // --- winter transitions (timer only; mechanics = Phase 10) ---
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4238:            !_world.winterActive && newTick >= _world.winterStartsAtTick
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4239:                && _world.winterStartsAtTick < _world.seasonEndTick
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4241:            _world.winterActive = true;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4244:        if (_world.winterActive && newTick >= _world.winterEndsAtTick) {
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4245:            _world.winterActive = false;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4247:            // schedule next winter cycle within this season
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4248:            uint64 nextWinterStart = _world.winterEndsAtTick + ClanWorldConstants.TICKS_PER_WINTER_CYCLE
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4250:            uint64 nextWinterEnd = _world.winterEndsAtTick + ClanWorldConstants.TICKS_PER_WINTER_CYCLE;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4252:                _world.winterStartsAtTick = nextWinterStart;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4253:                _world.winterEndsAtTick = nextWinterEnd;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4255:                // no more winters this season; sentinel = seasonEndTick so guard never fires
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4256:                _world.winterStartsAtTick = _world.seasonEndTick;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4257:                _world.winterEndsAtTick = _world.seasonEndTick;

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5012:The requested fixes mostly line up in the contract diff: starvation now uses `< tick`, wood burn is base plus per-clansman, winter events are `uint64`, wheat lock clears existing plots, and §7.3 is patched. The more interesting review risk is in the edges around newly derived ABI/view shapes and winter-time minting.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5015:The upkeep subreview caught a real miss: the new `< tick` guard fixes “fresh winter starts starving” but not “already starving before winter starts.” I’m doing one last pass on ABI/view consumers and wheat-lock behavior, then I’ll synthesize cleanly with severity.

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5559:Not clean. Three requested fixes verify cleanly: wood formula for a non-starving 4-clansman clan is `3e18`, winter events are back to `uint64`, existing wheat plots lock with `remainingWheat = 0` and `regrowUntilTick = 0`, and spec §7.3 is patched.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5561:Two HIGHs remain: starvation timing still differs for pre-winter starvers, and hardcoded TS ABIs are now out of sync with `WorldState` / `WorldSnapshot`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5565:1. **Pre-winter starvers still die one tick earlier than fresh winter starvers**
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5572:if (winter && starving && clan.starvationStartsAtTick < tick)
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5575:That defers death for a clan that starts starving on the first winter tick, but not for a clan already starving before winter. Example: winter starts at tick `110`; a pre-winter starver with `starvationStartsAtTick = 105` dies on tick `110`, while a fresh winter starver with `starvationStartsAtTick = 110` first dies on tick `111`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5577:The added test misses this because it sets both clans’ `lastSettledTick` after winter has already begun. The comparison needs an effective winter starvation start, e.g. `max(starvationStartsAtTick, currentWinterStartTick)`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5581:`packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol@1210e90:192` and `:414` include `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before winter fields. But:
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5591:1. **Wood burn uses post-starvation living count on the same tick**
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5595:If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated afterward from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation that tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18`. The requested non-starving case passes, but the same-tick ordering is a fairness wrinkle.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5597:2. **Clans minted during winter start `WinterLocked` with nonzero wheat**
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5601:Winter-minted plots get `state = WinterLocked`, but still initialize `remainingWheat = WHEAT_PLOT_STARTING_WHEAT`. Harvesting is blocked, so this is mostly a state/indexer invariant bug, but it violates the winter-lock triple expected for plots.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5610:- Existing wheat plot winter lock correctly sets `WinterLocked`, `remainingWheat = 0`, and `regrowUntilTick = 0` at `ClanWorld.sol@1210e90:1138-1141`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5623:Not clean. Three requested fixes verify cleanly: wood formula for a non-starving 4-clansman clan is `3e18`, winter events are back to `uint64`, existing wheat plots lock with `remainingWheat = 0` and `regrowUntilTick = 0`, and spec §7.3 is patched.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5625:Two HIGHs remain: starvation timing still differs for pre-winter starvers, and hardcoded TS ABIs are now out of sync with `WorldState` / `WorldSnapshot`.

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5639:That defers death for a clan that starts starving on the first winter tick, but not for a clan already starving before winter. Example: winter starts at tick `110`; a pre-winter starver with `starvationStartsAtTick = 105` dies on tick `110`, while a fresh winter starver with `starvationStartsAtTick = 110` first dies on tick `111`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5641:The added test misses this because it sets both clans’ `lastSettledTick` after winter has already begun. The comparison needs an effective winter starvation start, e.g. `max(starvationStartsAtTick, currentWinterStartTick)`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5645:`packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol@1210e90:192` and `:414` include `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before winter fields. But:
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5655:1. **Wood burn uses post-starvation living count on the same tick**
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5659:If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated afterward from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation that tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18`. The requested non-starving case passes, but the same-tick ordering is a fairness wrinkle.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5661:2. **Clans minted during winter start `WinterLocked` with nonzero wheat**
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5665:Winter-minted plots get `state = WinterLocked`, but still initialize `remainingWheat = WHEAT_PLOT_STARTING_WHEAT`. Harvesting is blocked, so this is mostly a state/indexer invariant bug, but it violates the winter-lock triple expected for plots.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5674:- Existing wheat plot winter lock correctly sets `WinterLocked`, `remainingWheat = 0`, and `regrowUntilTick = 0` at `ClanWorld.sol@1210e90:1138-1141`.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:61:**Settlement simulation (`_simulateSettleToTick`) is consistent with real settlement.** The starvation check uses `_world.currentTick` in both real and simulated paths — since all simulated ticks ≤ `_world.currentTick`, the inequality `starvationStartsAtTick <= _world.currentTick` produces the same result as real settlement. The simulation correctly deactivates dead clansmen's missions without attempting reservation refunds (which would require state mutation incompatible with `view`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:177:         return clan.starvationStartsAtTick != 0 && clan.starvationStartsAtTick <= _world.currentTick;

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3511:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10260:23f7f1a:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:280:        uint64 winterStart = world.getWorldState().winterStartsAtTick;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3694:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5260:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5261:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.

The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6202:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5925:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6904:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10601:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6906:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10643:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6919:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7008:docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7239:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7241:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:157:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7242:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:175:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.

Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7283:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.

docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:26:| H5 | `ClanWorld.sol:544-559` (`_markClansmanDead`), `:565-591` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = 1/3 | HIGH | **Cold-damage + starvation deaths DON'T refund upgrade reservations.** `_markClansmanDead` sets `cs.state = DEAD; m.active = false;` immediately. The death-path refund branch in `_settleMissionForClansman:349-364` gates on `if (m.active)` — already false. Permanent vault resource loss + `_pending*UpgradesByClan` counter never decrements → eventually blocks ALL future upgrades. **Phase 4.4 winter cold damage already ships this regression.** Fix: extract `_invalidateActiveMission` helper that calls refund THEN sets m.active=false. Apply in `_markClansmanDead` + `_markClanDead`. |
docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:54:- 1/3 only: H5 (death-refund orphan — but CRITICAL because reachable via shipped Phase 4.4 winter), H6 (level-binding mismatch)
docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:58:**Recommend Path A (fix-round) — high confidence.** Phase 8 has the most multi-model HIGH overlap of any phase super-swarm so far + 1 critical single-model finding (H5 death-refund) that's already shipping resource-loss bugs via Phase 4.4 winter. Fix scope:

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4668:   989	                && _world.winterStartsAtTick < _world.seasonEndTick
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4670:   991	            _world.winterActive = true;

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4677:   998	            uint64 nextWinterStart = _world.winterEndsAtTick + ClanWorldConstants.TICKS_PER_WINTER_CYCLE
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4679:  1000	            uint64 nextWinterEnd = _world.winterEndsAtTick + ClanWorldConstants.TICKS_PER_WINTER_CYCLE;

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5352:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7562:getWorldSnapshot ["currentTick:uint64","seasonStartTick:uint64","seasonEndTick:uint64","seasonFinalized:bool","currentSeasonNumber:uint64","nextHeartbeatAtTick:uint64","winterActive:bool","winterStartsAtTick:uint64","winterEndsAtTick:uint64","activeBanditId:uint32","currentTickSeed:bytes32","leaderboard:tuple[]"]
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5368:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5370:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5492:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10601:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5493:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10643:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5629:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2849:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5053:    ///         Drives top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5633:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5634:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3301:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.

This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7282:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5911:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7284:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5914:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7409:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7410:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7524:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6904:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10601:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7525:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6906:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10643:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9815:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3867:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14957:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9816:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3869:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14999:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9817:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3871:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:23:| H3 | `ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Winter starvation replay bug.** `_applyUpkeep` + `_simulateApplyUpkeep` read `_world.winterActive` (current flag) while replaying historical ticks. A clan that starved during a past winter but settled after winter ends will skip those deaths in both mutating settlement AND derived getters. Fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick`, not the current world flag. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9828:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3997:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3867:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14957:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11119:b335e9c feat(contracts+convex): Phase 4.4 — winter/season timers (Closes #169) (#175)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11325:   811	2. update starvation status if shortage occurred
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11370:    84	- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11402:   947	- walls may lose levels from winter cold damage and bandit pressure
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:But three real issues land: (1) **cold-damage RNG uses `_world.currentTickSeed` instead of the historical `_tickSeeds[tick]`**, which the rest of the codebase consistently uses for lazy-settle RNG — this breaks lazy-settle determinism and is timing-exploitable; (2) **starvation kill condition has an inverted-fairness guard** that grants pre-winter-starvers immunity from winter starvation kills; (3) **wood burn semantics deviate from spec** (per-clansman 0.5e18 vs spec's per-base 1e18). Plus a handful of MEDIUM/LOW smaller issues. Recommend NOT merging until at least the first two are addressed (or explicitly resolved as design changes with an ADR/spec patch).
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:31:### H2. Starvation kill guard grants pre-winter starvers immunity
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:35:(, uint64 winterStartsAtTick,) = _winterWindowForTick(tick);
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:36:if (winter && starving && clan.starvationStartsAtTick >= winterStartsAtTick) {
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:41:Trace: clan A starves continuously since tick 105 → `starvationStartsAtTick = 105`. Winter window is `[110, 120)`. For ticks 110-119, condition evaluates `winter=true && starving=true && (105 >= 110)` = **false**. **No clansmen die from starvation.** Meanwhile clan B that just barely tipped into starvation at tick 112 has `starvationStartsAtTick = 112 >= 110` and loses a clansman every tick.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:43:**Inverted fairness.** A clan that has been starving longer should die *faster*, not be immune. Worse, this is gameable: an Elder who anticipates running out of food in winter can deliberately start starving pre-winter to claim immunity. Spec §4.12/v4.1 A10 is silent on the exact in-winter starvation cadence (only "outside winter, starvation does not directly kill"), so the guard isn't required by spec.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:45:**Fix.** Drop the `starvationStartsAtTick >= winterStartsAtTick` clause and just gate on `winter && starving`. (Or, if there's a design reason for a "settle-in" delay, use `tick >= winterStartsAtTick + N` rather than tying it to the starvation start tick.) Add a regression test that pre-winter-starves a clan and asserts it loses clansmen during winter at the same cadence as a fresh-winter-starver.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:### M2. Wheat plot regrow lost at winter-start boundary
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:64:`_lockWheatPlotsForWinter` *unconditionally* sets every plot's `state = WinterLocked` regardless of pre-existing state. If a plot was `Regrowing` with `regrowUntilTick <= 109` (pre-winter), and the clan didn't trigger `_settleClan` in the [regrow-end, winter-start] window, the lazy regrow check (`if (plot.state == WheatPlotState.Regrowing && tick >= plot.regrowUntilTick)`) at line 379 will never see `Regrowing` — it's already been overwritten to `WinterLocked` by the heartbeat. The "should-have-been-Harvestable" tick is silently dropped.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:66:Practical impact is bounded: post-winter `_restartWheatPlotsAfterWinter` resets all plots to a fresh `Regrowing(winterEnd + 4)`, so the only damage is lost harvest *opportunity* in the pre-winter regrow window for clans that didn't settle. With orchestrator polling every tick the window is small. Still, fairness asymmetry between settled/unsettled clans is real.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:70:### M3. Orphan `ClansmanDiedFromCold` event in interface
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:75:event ClansmanDiedFromCold(uint32 indexed clanId, uint64 atTick);             // never emitted
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:78:Both events are exported in the ABI (`abi/IClanWorld.json:2974, 2999`). The old name is dead — no `emit ClansmanDiedFromCold(...)` anywhere. This is interface bloat, but worse, any indexer/frontend code that subscribed to the old event will silently receive zero notifications going forward.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:80:**Fix.** Remove `ClansmanDiedFromCold` from `IClanWorldEvents`. Audit `apps/server/convex/*` and `apps/web/*` for any reference. (Indexer in this repo, so blast radius is small — but worth the grep before merge.)
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:92:The motivation appears to be aligning with `_eventTick` (uint32 cast guarded by `require(tick <= type(uint32).max)`). With ~4B ticks the cast is safe in practice but the saved bytes don't justify the ABI churn — flag for design review whether the entire winter event family should standardize on uint64 (as `TickAdvanced` and `SeasonFinalized` do).
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:117:The require makes the soft-error path only reachable when a clan transitions DEAD *during* `_settleClan`. That's correct (lazy starvation/cold death mid-settle should not revert the caller's batch). But Test B (`test_deadClanCannotSubmitClanOrders`) only covers the revert side; there is **no test** that the soft-error path actually fires for a clan that dies during `_settleClan`. Add one.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:143:`ClanWorld.sol:1161-1165`. Iterates all clans, including DEAD ones. Resetting `coldDamage = 0` on a dead clan is harmless (the field is unread for dead clans). Wasted gas at winter-end heartbeat. Add `if (clan.clanState == ClanState.DEAD) continue;`. Same for `_lockWheatPlotsForWinter` and `_restartWheatPlotsAfterWinter` (lines 1132-1158) — locking/unlocking a dead clan's plots writes to slots that will never be read again. Trivial gas cleanup.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:146:`ClanWorld.sol:80, 1137, 1149`. Hardcoded for "24 clans × 2 plots" but the project ships with 8 clans (per `docs/planning/V1/07 ... v4.5 ... §3.1`). If anyone ever bumps `MAX_CLANS` past 24 without bumping this constant, the heartbeat that crosses winter will revert and the world freezes. Either make the cap a function of `MAX_CLANS`, or downgrade the `require` to a "cap = process up to N this tick, defer the rest" pattern (mirroring `MAX_MARKET_ACTIONS_PER_TICK`). Since the plot transition needs to be atomic at the boundary, the deferred approach doesn't fit cleanly — at minimum, add an invariant test `assertLe(MAX_CLANS * 2, MAX_CROP_TRANSITION_PER_TICK)`.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:152:The test suite tests winter onset, end, and the next cycle, plus crop locks/restart, plus all death paths. **Missing** regression tests for:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:155:- Pre-winter starvation kill behavior (H2) — clan starving since tick 100, winter at 110, assert clansmen die in winter.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:156:- Plot regrow loss at boundary (M2) — harvest at tick 105, do not settle, advance through winter, assert (or document) the lost mid-summer regrow.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:169:1. **The `_markClanDead` helper is good infrastructure.** Both starvation and cold death route through it; defenders are cleared; vault wipe is consistent; both `ClanEliminated` (legacy) and `ClanDied(reason)` are emitted (good for off-chain consumer migration). Phase 9.4 attack-final-kill should plug into this pathway via `_markClansmanDead` followed by the existing `if (livingClansmen == 0) _markClanDead(...)` check — keep that pattern uniform.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:173:3. **`_world.winterActive` storage field is now dead.** It's set to `false` at construction (line 97, `ClanWorldStub.sol:54`) and *never written again*. The view derives `winterActive` from `_winterWindowForTick`. This is fine but confusing — consider removing the field from `WorldState` storage (keep it only in the view struct) so future readers don't get tripped up. (ABI-stable: `WorldState` is exported via `getWorldState()` so removal is an ABI break — defer to a coordinated state-layout rework.)
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:175:4. **Heartbeat ordering is correct (Step 1 settle → Step 4 winter transition → Step 5 tick increment).** Mission settlements at the boundary close-tick run against pre-lock plot state, which is what you'd want. The boundary fragility is in lazy-settle plot state (M2), not heartbeat ordering.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:68:+Winter occurs every `110` ticks. The first winter opens at tick `110`, so ticks `[100, 110)` remain pre-winter preparation time.

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1403:+    function test_winter_cropTransitions_lockThenRestartRegrow() public {
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1415:+        uint64 winterStart = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1416:+        _advanceToTick(winterStart - 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1421:+        assertEq(uint8(west1.state), uint8(WheatPlotState.WinterLocked), "clan1 west locks at winter start");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1422:+        assertEq(uint8(east1.state), uint8(WheatPlotState.WinterLocked), "clan1 east locks at winter start");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1423:+        assertEq(uint8(west2.state), uint8(WheatPlotState.WinterLocked), "clan2 west locks at winter start");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1424:+        assertEq(uint8(east2.state), uint8(WheatPlotState.WinterLocked), "clan2 east locks at winter start");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1425:+        assertEq(west1.remainingWheat, 0, "winter lock clears remaining wheat");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1426:+        assertEq(east1.remainingWheat, 0, "winter lock clears all plots");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1427:+        assertEq(west1.regrowUntilTick, 0, "winter lock clears regrow tick");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1428:+        assertEq(east2.regrowUntilTick, 0, "winter lock clears all regrow ticks");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1432:+        assertEq(uint8(results[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_WINTER_LOCKED), "harvest locked during winter");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1434:+        uint64 winterEnd = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK + ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_DURATION_TICKS;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1435:+        _advanceToTick(winterEnd - 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1438:+        uint64 expectedRegrowUntil = winterEnd + ClanWorldConstants.WHEAT_PLOT_REGROW_TICKS;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1445:+        assertEq(west1.remainingWheat, 0, "winter end clears remaining wheat");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1446:+        assertEq(east2.remainingWheat, 0, "winter end clears all plots");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1447:+        assertEq(west1.regrowUntilTick, expectedRegrowUntil, "west regrow restarts from winter end");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1448:+        assertEq(east2.regrowUntilTick, expectedRegrowUntil, "east regrow restarts from winter end");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1464:+    function test_winterLockedPlotSettlesInFlightHarvestWithNoYield() public {

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1485:+    function test_winter_upkeep_doublesFoodAndBurnsWood() public {
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1489:+        uint64 winterStart = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1490:+        _advanceToTick(winterStart + 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1493:+        harness.setClanUpkeepState(clanId, winterStart, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 0);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1498:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWheat, 92e18, "winter wheat upkeep should be 2x");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1499:+        assertEq(clan.vaultFish, 100e18 - 8e17, "winter fish upkeep should be 2x");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1500:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 97e18, "winter wood burn should include base and per clansman");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1501:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 0, "sufficient wood should avoid cold damage");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1506:+    function test_winter_upkeep_insufficientWood_emitsColdShortage() public {
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1510:+        uint64 winterStart = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1511:+        _advanceToTick(winterStart + 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1513:+        harness.setClanUpkeepState(clanId, winterStart, 1e18, 100e18, 100e18, 0);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1516:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ClanColdShortage(clanId, winterStart, 2e18);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1520:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "short winter burn should consume remaining wood");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1521:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 1, "short winter burn should mark cold damage");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1528:+        uint64 winterStart = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1529:+        _advanceToTick(winterStart + 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1532:+        harness.setClanUpkeepState(clanId, winterStart, 0, 100e18, 100e18, 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1535:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.WallDegradedByCold(clanId, 1, winterStart);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1539:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 2, "cold damage should increment");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1540:+        assertEq(clan.wallLevel, 1, "second cold damage should degrade wall by one");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1548:+        uint64 winterStart = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1549:+        _advanceToTick(winterStart + 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1551:+        harness.setClanUpkeepState(clanId, winterStart, 0, 100e18, 100e18, 1);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1558:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 2, "cold damage should hit death threshold");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1573:+    function test_pre_winter_starver_dies_in_winter_at_same_cadence() public {
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1577:+        uint32 winterStarver = _mintClan();
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1578:+        uint64 winterStart = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_START_TICK;

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1590:+        assertEq(preWinterClan.livingClansmen, 3, "pre-winter starver should skip first winter tick death");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1591:+        assertEq(winterClan.livingClansmen, 3, "fresh winter starver should match pre-winter cadence");

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1859:I’ll treat this as a final-review pass and use the repo as ground truth around the diff, especially the settlement/winter state paths and ABI consumers. I’m also going to split a couple of focused checks to subagents so the integration seams get more eyes.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1865:While those two reviewers dig, I’m reading the changed surfaces locally: contract state transitions first, then the TypeScript ABI consumers. The main thing I want to verify is whether “derived winter state” and “stored world state” stay consistent across heartbeat, settlement, and views.

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4857:I’ve got the head commit available as `origin/dev-phase-10-winter`, so I’m using `git show 400463e:…` to inspect the exact merged state without touching the worktree. One promising seam is the ordering between heartbeat-driven work and lazy clan upkeep/death; that’s where the winter mechanics change the old assumptions most.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7952:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5368:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7965:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5633:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8874:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3226:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5202:Reviewing the fix-round diff against the surrounding contract and TS client code now. I’m going to split this into parallel passes: one on the Solidity bandit/heartbeat path, one on ABI/TypeScript decode safety, and one on the new tests and starvation replay logic so we can verify the five prior HIGHs and look for regressions.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8875:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3227:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5203:  → Inspect changed Solidity bandit/heartbeat/starvation code in context and identify behavioral regressions
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8878:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3297:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8879:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8880:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3301:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8881:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3351:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4585:    46	///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8882:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8892:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3548:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8893:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3552:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8894:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3553:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8928:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3770:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:    48	///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8929:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8930:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8931:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3872:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8932:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3876:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8977:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2033:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:47:///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8978:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2149:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5368:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8981:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2159:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5627:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2797:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8982:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2164:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5633:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9018:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3079:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2675:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9019:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3181:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2796:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9020:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3182:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2797:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9021:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3498:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3226:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5202:Reviewing the fix-round diff against the surrounding contract and TS client code now. I’m going to split this into parallel passes: one on the Solidity bandit/heartbeat path, one on ABI/TypeScript decode safety, and one on the new tests and starvation replay logic so we can verify the five prior HIGHs and look for regressions.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9022:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3499:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3227:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5203:  → Inspect changed Solidity bandit/heartbeat/starvation code in context and identify behavioral regressions
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9023:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3553:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3297:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9024:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3554:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9025:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3556:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3301:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9026:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3592:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3351:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4585:    46	///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9027:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3667:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9037:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3748:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3548:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9038:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3752:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3552:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9039:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3753:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3553:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9053:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3788:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3600:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9054:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3789:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3601:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9055:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3792:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3606:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6919:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9056:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3793:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3607:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9057:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3794:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3609:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7008:docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9058:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3795:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3610:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7068:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:84:- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9059:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3803:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3620:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7249:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9060:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3807:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3624:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7282:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
