The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9064:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3825:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3644:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:47:///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9065:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3904:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3743:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4859:///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9066:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3923:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3770:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:    48	///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9067:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3996:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9068:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3998:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9069:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4002:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3872:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9070:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4006:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3876:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9102:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5294:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9103:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5295:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9104:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5297:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9105:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5382:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5202:Reviewing the fix-round diff against the surrounding contract and TS client code now. I’m going to split this into parallel passes: one on the Solidity bandit/heartbeat path, one on ABI/TypeScript decode safety, and one on the new tests and starvation replay logic so we can verify the five prior HIGHs and look for regressions.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9106:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5383:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5203:  → Inspect changed Solidity bandit/heartbeat/starvation code in context and identify behavioral regressions
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9107:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5441:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9108:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5442:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9109:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5444:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9110:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5622:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5643:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9111:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5624:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5925:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9112:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5644:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9113:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9114:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5689:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4585:    46	///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9115:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5809:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4859:///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9116:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5828:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:    48	///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9117:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5903:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9118:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5905:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9119:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5915:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9120:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5919:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9121:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5920:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9163:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7156:3455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9164:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7158:3553:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9165:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7159:3581:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5911:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9166:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7160:3582:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5914:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9167:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7161:3600:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9168:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7162:3601:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9169:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7163:3607:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9170:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7166:3632:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8190:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9171:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7167:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9172:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7168:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9198:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7224:3788:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3600:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9199:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7225:3789:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3601:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9200:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7226:3793:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3607:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9201:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7229:3815:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3632:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8190:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9202:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7230:3996:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9203:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7231:3998:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10529:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10662:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10978:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5457:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:84:- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10997:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5911:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10999:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5914:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11011:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6195:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5643:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11012:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6202:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5925:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11029:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11031:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11058:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6919:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11059:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11062:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7008:docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id

Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11548:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:    48	///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12821:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12827:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12899:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12906:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4233:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4341:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:22:The first winter begins at **tick 110**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4234:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4342:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:24:Thereafter, winter begins every 110 ticks:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4235:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4343:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:30:Each winter lasts **10 ticks**, as already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4236:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4344:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:81:## A6. Just-in-time winter logistics are intentionally punitive
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4237:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4345:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:85:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4238:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4346:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:91:This means that during winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4239:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4347:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4240:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4348:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:156:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4241:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4349:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:158:This is intentional and follows the existing rule that starvation reduces all clansmen defense contribution to zero.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4242:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4350:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:166:## A10. No change to summer starvation lethality
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4243:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4351:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:168:Starvation outside winter does **not** directly kill clansmen.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4244:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4352:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:170:While starving outside winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4245:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4353:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:174:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4246:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4354:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:176:This is intentional for v1 to avoid excessively punishing early-game elimination.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4247:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4357:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_master_coordination.md:115:Backend spec §12 asks: when `heartbeat()` runs, does `TickAdvanced` fire **before** or **after** other tick-end side effects (bandit attacks, scheduled markets, winter transitions)?
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4248:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4358:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4249:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4359:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4250:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4361:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:353:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4251:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4362:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:402:- starvation checks

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4254:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4365:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:484:Outside Winter, starvation does **not** directly kill clansmen in v1.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4255:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4366:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:486:## 4.13 Lazy starvation tracking

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4316:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4437:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:418:- at winter end: `WinterLocked -> Regrowing`
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4318:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4441:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:811:2. update starvation status if shortage occurred
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4319:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4442:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4365:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4501:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:22:The first winter begins at **tick 110**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4366:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4502:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:24:Thereafter, winter begins every 110 ticks:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4367:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4503:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:30:Each winter lasts **10 ticks**, as already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4368:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4504:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:81:## A6. Just-in-time winter logistics are intentionally punitive
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4369:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4505:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:85:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4370:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4506:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:91:This means that during winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4371:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4507:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4372:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4508:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:157:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4373:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4509:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:159:This is intentional and follows the existing rule that starvation reduces all clansmen defense contribution to zero.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4374:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4510:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:167:## A10. No change to summer starvation lethality

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4376:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4512:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:171:While starving outside winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4377:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4513:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:175:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4378:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4514:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:177:This is intentional for v1 to avoid excessively punishing early-game elimination.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4504:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:20:## A2. First winter timing
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4505:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:22:The first winter begins at **tick 110**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4506:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:24:Thereafter, winter begins every 110 ticks:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4507:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:30:Each winter lasts **10 ticks**, as already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4508:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:81:## A6. Just-in-time winter logistics are intentionally punitive
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4509:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:85:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4510:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:91:This means that during winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4511:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4512:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:157:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4513:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:159:This is intentional and follows the existing rule that starvation reduces all clansmen defense contribution to zero.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4514:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:167:## A10. No change to summer starvation lethality
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4515:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:169:Starvation outside winter does **not** directly kill clansmen.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4516:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:171:While starving outside winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4517:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:175:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4518:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:177:This is intentional for v1 to avoid excessively punishing early-game elimination.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4519:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4520:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4521:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:353:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4526:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:484:Outside Winter, starvation does **not** directly kill clansmen in v1.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4527:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:486:## 4.13 Lazy starvation tracking
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4528:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:487:To keep heartbeat cheap, starvation should not require full clan mutation every tick.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4529:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:490:- store starvation threshold timing or equivalent compact status data
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4530:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:491:- interpret starvation status lazily at clan settlement time and in views
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4531:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:492:- do not force heartbeat-based full clan settlement solely to flip starvation state
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4532:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:832:During winter, each clan consumes:

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4536:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:845:- both plots restart regrowing after winter ends
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4537:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:846:- both plots become harvestable again `4` ticks after winter ends
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4538:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:849:During winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4539:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:854:## 7.6 Winter failure: cold damage
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4540:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:855:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4541:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:868:Cold damage does **not** persist across winters.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4542:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:870:Wall damage and clansman deaths caused by earlier winter cold still persist, but the temporary cold-damage accumulator does not carry into the next winter cycle.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4543:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:947:- walls may lose levels from winter cold damage and bandit pressure
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4545:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:991:This is approximately three winters under the current winter cadence.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4551:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1102:These values are intended to prevent instant early starvation while still forcing quick economic action.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4555:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1189:- starvation weakens but does not kill outside Winter
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4556:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1194:- winter every 110 ticks for 10 ticks
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4557:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1195:- cold damage reset each winter
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4559:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:43:- winter cadence and cold damage
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4560:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:46:- elimination state and payout ranking inputs
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4561:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:69:This is required so target selection, defense totals, starvation state, and vault values are correct at attack resolution time.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4629:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:20:## A2. First winter timing
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4630:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:22:The first winter begins at **tick 110**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4631:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:24:Thereafter, winter begins every 110 ticks:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4632:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:30:Each winter lasts **10 ticks**, as already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4633:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:81:## A6. Just-in-time winter logistics are intentionally punitive
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4634:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:85:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4635:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:91:This means that during winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4636:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4637:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:156:If a clansman from Clan A is defending Clan B's base, and Clan A enters starvation, that defending clansman contributes **0 defense** while starvation is active, even though they are physically present at Clan B's base.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4638:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:158:This is intentional and follows the existing rule that starvation reduces all clansmen defense contribution to zero.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4639:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:166:## A10. No change to summer starvation lethality
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4640:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:168:Starvation outside winter does **not** directly kill clansmen.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4641:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:170:While starving outside winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4642:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:174:Clansman death from deprivation occurs only through the winter cold-damage / wall-collapse pathway already specified in v4.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4643:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:176:This is intentional for v1 to avoid excessively punishing early-game elimination.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4647:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4648:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4649:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:353:2. update starvation status for the next tick if shortages occur

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4654:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:484:Outside Winter, starvation does **not** directly kill clansmen in v1.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4655:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:486:## 4.13 Lazy starvation tracking
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4656:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:487:To keep heartbeat cheap, starvation should not require full clan mutation every tick.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4657:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:490:- store starvation threshold timing or equivalent compact status data
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4658:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:491:- interpret starvation status lazily at clan settlement time and in views
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4659:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:492:- do not force heartbeat-based full clan settlement solely to flip starvation state
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4660:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:832:During winter, each clan consumes:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4661:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:838:During winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4662:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:841:- regrowth does not complete during winter
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4663:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:843:At winter start:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4664:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:845:- both plots restart regrowing after winter ends
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4665:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:846:- both plots become harvestable again `4` ticks after winter ends
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4666:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:849:During winter:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4667:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:854:## 7.6 Winter failure: cold damage
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4668:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:855:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4669:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:868:Cold damage does **not** persist across winters.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4670:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:870:Wall damage and clansman deaths caused by earlier winter cold still persist, but the temporary cold-damage accumulator does not carry into the next winter cycle.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4671:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:947:- walls may lose levels from winter cold damage and bandit pressure
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4673:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:991:This is approximately three winters under the current winter cadence.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4679:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1102:These values are intended to prevent instant early starvation while still forcing quick economic action.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4683:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1189:- starvation weakens but does not kill outside Winter

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4685:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1195:- cold damage reset each winter

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5012:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1924:Local files aren’t at the PR head, so I’m treating the pasted diff as authoritative and using the repo mainly for surrounding contracts/spec context. One thing already stands out: this PR changes the winter start model from the older `[100,110)` style to `WINTER_START_TICK = 110`, so I’m checking whether that matches the phase spec rather than assuming it’s intentional.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5294:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5295:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5297:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5441:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5442:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5443:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8313:  - Winter starvation replay is using `_isWinterTick(tick)` in both storage settlement and view simulation.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5444:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5445:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8349:  - Winter starvation replay is using `_isWinterTick(tick)` in both storage settlement and view simulation.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5642:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:22:| H2 | `runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts:34-51` + `shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34-58` | 5.4 + 5.5 = **2/5** | HIGH | **Stale handwritten ABI tuples.** `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` inserted `currentSeasonNumber` + `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `nextHeartbeatAtTs`/`winterActive`, but TS clients still use old layout. Will misdecode heartbeat timestamps and break heartbeat automation at deploy. Fix: regenerate from generated ABI imports, or hand-fix both tuples. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5643:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:23:| H3 | `ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Winter starvation replay bug.** `_applyUpkeep` + `_simulateApplyUpkeep` read `_world.winterActive` (current flag) while replaying historical ticks. A clan that starved during a past winter but settled after winter ends will skip those deaths in both mutating settlement AND derived getters. Fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick`, not the current world flag. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5644:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5645:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:35:| M3 | `ClanWorld.sol:1206` (`_simulateResolveAction`) | 4.6 = **1/5** | MED | **Starvation check uses `_world.currentTick` instead of simulation tick.** Latent bug — current control flow prevents misalignment, but breaks if simulation structure changes. Use `sim.clan.starvationStartsAtTick <= tick`. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5646:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:64:- **Codex 5.4:** NEEDS_FIXES — 4 HIGH (incl. ABI staleness + winter replay + blueprint unit) + multiple LOW
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5647:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:75:- Single-model findings: **rest** — including 3 of the 5 HIGH (winter replay, blueprint unit, attack-revert-on-death)
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5649:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:51:### M2: `_simulateResolveAction` starvation check uses `_world.currentTick` instead of simulation tick
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5650:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:56:bool starving = sim.clan.starvationStartsAtTick != 0 
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5651:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:57:    && sim.clan.starvationStartsAtTick <= _world.currentTick;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5652:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5653:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:62:**Impact:** Low in practice — the upkeep/resolution ordering within the simulation loop prevents the misalignment. But it's a latent bug if the simulation structure changes. Should use `sim.clan.starvationStartsAtTick <= tick` for correctness parity.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5762:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10334:23f7f1a:packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2046:        assertEq(world.getWorldState().currentTick, winterStart, "currentTick should be 100");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5763:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10601:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5765:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10643:`packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:34` — `RealChainClient` still declares the old `getWorldSnapshot()` tuple shape. The contract `WorldSnapshot` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `winterActive` at `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:407`, but the hand-written viem ABI jumps from `seasonFinalized` to `winterActive`. This can decode the dynamic `leaderboard` offset incorrectly and break `getCurrentTick()`. Update/import the generated ABI and add a decode test for the minimal client ABI.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5903:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5904:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14957:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5905:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5906:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14999:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5908:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:177:         return clan.starvationStartsAtTick != 0 && clan.starvationStartsAtTick <= _world.currentTick;
